stocks.recent_news: Nippon India ETF Gold BeES trading around Rs 123.74 on 11-May-2026, down 0.46% intraday. AUM Rs 54,127.54 cr (Apr 29, 2026); expense ratio 0.80%. May 2026 forecast shows modest near-term softness (~-0.9% month) but firm medium-term gold outlook. ETF tracks domestic physical gold; no company-specific newsflow as it is a passive vehicle.
stocks.sector_context: Gold supported by RBI rate-cut cycle (lower real rates favor non-yielding assets), continued central-bank reserve accumulation, de-dollarisation, expectations of further US Fed rate cuts, and geopolitical risk premium. WGC Q1 2026 India focus shows investment demand still firm. ETF AUM continues to grow, reflecting structural investor allocation.
stocks.peer_comparison: GOLDBEES is the largest and most liquid gold ETF in India; returns mirror physical gold so dispersion vs HDFC/ICICI/Kotak/SBI gold ETFs is minimal aside from tracking error and expense ratio. Mid-pack expense ratio at 0.80%.
red_flags: Expense drag of 0.80% versus Sovereign Gold Bonds which add 2.5% coupon
Near-term consensus implies a slightly negative month for gold
Tracking error can widen during periods of physical gold volatility
catalysts: US Fed rate decisions and DXY trajectory
Continued RBI / EM central bank gold purchases
INR weakness translating to higher domestic gold price

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 results (May 5-6, 2026): consolidated net profit up ~18% YoY to Rs 408 cr; total income up 22.2% YoY to Rs 3,393 cr. India business volume growth 9%; international constant-currency growth ~19%. Board recommended final dividend of Rs 4 per share. Stock initially dipped ~2.3% then surged ~4.5% to a record high after results. MOFSL maintained Buy with TP Rs 950 (~15% upside).
stocks.sector_context: FMCG sector flagging three headwinds: geopolitical tensions, monsoon uncertainty, and crude-linked input cost pressure. HUL, Dabur and Nestle signaling calibrated price hikes. Sector still expected to grow 9-11% CAGR led by rural recovery and premiumisation; recent sector momentum positive into May.
stocks.peer_comparison: Marico's 9% India volume growth and 22% revenue growth are at the top of large-cap FMCG print this season (HUL volume ~6%). International outperformance is best-in-class. Valuation is premium to the FMCG basket.
red_flags: Premium FMCG valuation - record-high price reduces margin of safety
Copra, edible oil and crude-linked input costs remain volatile
International business carries FX translation exposure
catalysts: 38th AGM scheduled August 6, 2026 with FY27 outlook commentary
Q1 FY27 print on rural demand and pricing pass-through

stocks.recent_news: Q4 FY26 (May 6, 2026): consolidated net profit jumped ~134% YoY to Rs 612 cr (aided by a major one-time tax benefit + strong operations); sales up 30% YoY to Rs 4,450 cr. FY26 PAT up 91.6% to Rs 1,556 cr; revenue up 35.9% to Rs 16,403 cr. $2.5B Encora acquisition completed; financial consolidation effective May 1, 2026 (FY27 will reflect ~11 months of Encora). Stock surged ~10% post-results; trading near Rs 1,285 on May 7. Choice IE Buy with TP Rs 1,900 (~47% upside).
stocks.sector_context: Indian IT mixed - Goldman Sachs downgraded TCS, Infosys, Tech Mahindra; upgraded Wipro on valuations. Sector facing 'AI deflation' (3-5% revenue drag), softer BFSI/telecom discretionary, longer deal cycles. Pipeline still strong (record TCV wins; FY26 graduate hiring ~82,000). Mid-cap AI-engineering specialists outperforming large-cap services.
stocks.peer_comparison: Q4 revenue +30% YoY materially above large-cap IT (low/mid single-digit). FY26 revenue growth +35.9% places Coforge among the best-performing mid-caps. Encora deal vaults Coforge into a higher scale bracket and deepens AI/data engineering capabilities.
red_flags: Q4 profit growth flattered by one-time tax benefit - underlying run-rate is lower
$2.5B Encora integration is large and execution-sensitive
Higher leverage post-acquisition may pressure balance sheet
Stock has run hard into Q4 print - near-term overbought
catalysts: FY27 guidance with full Encora consolidation
Q1 FY27 first quarter of combined entity
Order book / TCV cadence and large-deal announcements

stocks.recent_news: Trading ~Rs 2,738-2,780 in early May 2026. Q3 FY26 (Dec 2025) net profit rose 59.6% YoY to Rs 432.7 cr on 6% revenue growth. Management projected 15-20% growth in chemicals business, which earlier drove a ~9% stock pop. Board approved new pharma intermediates plant at Dahej (Rs 180 cr capex) and declared an interim dividend of Rs 5/share.
stocks.sector_context: Indian specialty chemicals market USD 62.8B (2024) -> USD 95B by 2030 (~4.8% CAGR). Tailwinds from China+1, PLI schemes, agrochem capex, green chemistry. Deloitte flagged that the global chemical downturn (overcapacity, soft demand) may persist through 2026. Peer Q4 prints (e.g. Aarti +42.7% PAT) show selective recovery.
stocks.peer_comparison: SRF's diversified portfolio (fluorochemicals, specialty chems, packaging films, technical textiles) gives more resilience than pure-play specialty peers. Q3 PAT growth of 59.6% outpaces most large-cap chemical names. Fluorochemicals leadership remains a structural moat.
red_flags: Global chemical overcapacity / soft demand may persist through 2026
Packaging films and refrigerant cycles can be volatile
Q4 FY26 print not yet released - upside partially priced in
Multi-segment exposure means weak legs can offset strong ones
catalysts: Dahej pharma intermediates plant commissioning timeline
Refrigerant (HFC/HFO) pricing and US trade dynamics
Chemicals segment delivering on 15-20% growth guidance

stocks.recent_news: Trading near 52-week high around Rs 9,072 on 11-May-2026. Q4 FY26: PAT up 7% YoY (and 24.7% QoQ) to Rs 785.6 cr; revenue up 26.9% YoY to Rs 8,864.5 cr; EBITDA up 13.2% YoY to Rs 1,161 cr. FY26: PAT up 32.3% to Rs 2,672 cr on revenue up 28.9% to Rs 28,884 cr. Wires & cables revenue +33% YoY to Rs 25,179 cr; FMEG revenue +25% to Rs 2,069 cr. Board recommended final dividend of Rs 47/share (470%).
stocks.sector_context: Indian wires & cables market USD 23.1B (2026), projected ~9% CAGR to USD 35.6B by 2031. Drivers: infra capex, real estate, power transmission upgrades, renewables, data centres. Polycab ~18% share, KEI ~9%, RR Kabel ~7%. Copper/aluminium price volatility remains a key swing factor.
stocks.peer_comparison: Polycab remains the clear segment leader with ~18% share, supported by a 4,000-strong distributor network. FY26 W&C revenue growth (+33%) materially outpaces sector CAGR; FMEG scaling though still margin-dilutive. Margins remain top-quartile in the cables peer group.
red_flags: Stock at 52-week high - rich valuation and limited cushion if Q1 FY27 disappoints
Q4 commentary cited geopolitical disruption, demand slowdown, channel destocking from raw material volatility
Q4 PAT growth (+7% YoY) lagged revenue (+27% YoY), implying some margin compression
FMEG still dilutes consolidated margin profile
catalysts: Final dividend Rs 47/share record date and AGM
Demand recovery commentary post Q4 channel destocking
Order momentum from infra and data-centre build-out
